Output
"{\"content\":\"1: import re\\n2: \\n3: import numpy as np\\n4: import pytest\\n5: \\n6: from pandas._libs import index as libindex\\n7: \\n8: import pandas as pd\\n9: \\n10: \\n11: @pytest.fixture(\\n12:     params=[\\n13:         (libindex.Int64Engine, np.int64),\\n14:         (libindex.Int32Engine, np.int32),\\n15:         (libindex.Int16Engine, np.int16),\\n16:         (libindex.Int8Engine, np.int8),\\n17:         (libindex.UInt64Engine, np.uint64),\\n18:         (libindex.UInt32Engine, np.uint32),\\n19:         (libindex.UInt16Engine, np.uint16),\\n20:         (libindex.UInt8Engine, np.uint8),\\n21:         (libindex.Float64Engine, np.float64),\\n22:         (libindex.Float32Engine, np.float32),\\n23:     ],\\n24:     ids=lambda x: x[0].__name__,\\n25: )\\n26: def numeric_indexing_engine_type_and_dtype(request):\\n27:     return request.param\\n28: \\n29: \\n30: class TestDatetimeEngine:\\n31:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n32:         \\\"scalar\\\",\\n33:         [\\n34:             pd.Timedelta(pd.Timestamp(\\\"2016-01-01\\\").asm8.view(\\\"m8[ns]\\\")),\\n35:             pd.Timestamp(\\\"2016-01-01\\\")._value,\\n36:             pd.Timestamp(\\\"2016-01-01\\\").to_pydatetime(),\\n37:             pd.Timestamp(\\\"2016-01-01\\\").to_datetime64(),\\n38:         ],\\n39:     )\\n40:     def test_not_contains_requires_timestamp(self, scalar):\\n41:         dti1 = pd.date_range(\\\"2016-01-01\\\", periods=3)\\n42:         dti2 = dti1.insert(1, pd.NaT)  # non-monotonic\\n43:         dti3 = dti1.insert(3, dti1[0])  # non-unique\\n44:         dti4 = pd.date_range(\\\"2016-01-01\\\", freq=\\\"ns\\\", periods=2_000_000)\\n45:         dti5 = dti4.insert(0, dti4[0])  # over size threshold, not unique\\n46: \\n47:         msg = \\\"|\\\".join([re.escape(str(scalar)), re.escape(repr(scalar))])\\n48:         for dti in [dti1, dti2, dti3, dti4, dti5]:\\n49:             with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=msg):\\n50:                 scalar in dti._engine\\n51: \\n52:             with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=msg):\\n53:                 dti._engine.get_loc(scalar)\\n54: \\n55: \\n56: class TestTimedeltaEngine:\\n57:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n58:         \\\"scalar\\\",\\n59:         [\\n60:             pd.Timestamp(pd.Timedelta(days=42).asm8.view(\\\"datetime64[ns]\\\")),\\n61:             pd.Timedelta(days=42)._value,\\n62:             pd.Timedelta(days=42).to_pytimedelta(),\\n63:             pd.Timedelta(days=42).to_timedelta64(),\\n64:         ],\\n65:     )\\n66:     def test_not_contains_requires_timedelta(self, scalar):\\n67:         tdi1 = pd.timedelta_range(\\\"42 days\\\", freq=\\\"9h\\\", periods=1234)\\n68:         tdi2 = tdi1.insert(1, pd.NaT)  # non-monotonic\\n69:         tdi3 = tdi1.insert(3, tdi1[0])  # non-unique\\n70:         tdi4 = pd.timedelta_range(\\\"42 days\\\", freq=\\\"ns\\\", periods=2_000_000)\\n71:         tdi5 = tdi4.insert(0, tdi4[0])  # over size threshold, not unique\\n72: \\n73:         msg = \\\"|\\\".join([re.escape(str(scalar)), re.escape(repr(scalar))])\\n74:         for tdi in [tdi1, tdi2, tdi3, tdi4, tdi5]:\\n75:             with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=msg):\\n76:                 scalar in tdi._engine\\n77: \\n78:             with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=msg):\\n79:                 tdi._engine.get_loc(scalar)\\n80: \\n81: \\n82: class TestNumericEngine:\\n83:     def test_is_monotonic(self, numeric_indexing_engine_type_and_dtype):\\n84:         engine_type, dtype = numeric_indexing_engine_type_and_dtype\\n85:         num = 1000\\n86:         arr = np.array([1] * num + [2] * num + [3] * num, dtype=dtype)\\n87: \\n88:         # monotonic increasing\\n89:         engine = engine_type(arr)\\n90:         assert engine.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n91:         assert engine.is_monotonic_decreasing is False\\n92: \\n93:         # monotonic decreasing\\n94:         engine = engine_type(arr[::-1])\\n95:         assert engine.is_monotonic_increasing is False\\n96:         assert engine.is_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n97: \\n98:         # neither monotonic increasing or decreasing\\n99:         arr = np.array([1] * num + [2] * num + [1] * num, dtype=dtype)\\n100:         engine = engine_type(arr[::-1])\\n101:         assert engine.is_monotonic_increasing is False\\n102:         assert engine.is_monotonic_decreasing is False\\n103: \\n104:     def test_is_unique(self, numeric_indexing_engine_type_and_dtype):\\n105:         engine_type, dtype = numeric_indexing_engine_type_and_dtype\\n106: \\n107:         # unique\\n108:         arr = np.array([1, 3, 2], dtype=dtype)\\n109:         engine = engine_type(arr)\\n110:         assert engine.is_unique is True\\n111: \\n112:         # not unique\\n113:         arr = np.array([1, 2, 1], dtype=dtype)\\n114:         engine = engine_type(arr)\\n115:         assert engine.is_unique is False\\n116: \\n117:     def test_get_loc(self, numeric_indexing_engine_type_and_dtype):\\n118:         engine_type, dtype = numeric_indexing_engine_type_and_dtype\\n119: \\n120:         # unique\\n121:         arr = np.array([1, 2, 3], dtype=dtype)\\n122:         engine = engine_type(arr)\\n123:         assert engine.get_loc(2) == 1\\n124: \\n125:         # monotonic\\n126:         num = 1000\\n127:         arr = np.array([1] * num + [2] * num + [3] * num, dtype=dtype)\\n128:         engine = engine_type(arr)\\n129:         assert engine.get_loc(2) == slice(1000, 2000)\\n130: \\n131:         # not monotonic\\n132:         arr = np.array([1, 2, 3] * num, dtype=dtype)\\n133:         engine = engine_type(arr)\\n134:         expected = np.array([False, True, False] * num, dtype=bool)\\n135:         result = engine.get_loc(2)\\n136:         assert (result == expected).all()\\n137: \\n138: \\n139: class TestObjectEngine:\\n140:     engine_type = libindex.ObjectEngine\\n141:     dtype = np.object_\\n142:     values = list(\\\"abc\\\")\\n143: \\n144:     def test_is_monotonic(self):\\n145:         num = 1000\\n146:         arr = np.array([\\\"a\\\"] * num + [\\\"a\\\"] * num + [\\\"c\\\"] * num, dtype=self.dtype)\\n147: \\n148:         # monotonic increasing\\n149:         engine = self.engine_type(arr)\\n150:         assert engine.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n151:         assert engine.is_monotonic_decreasing is False\\n152: \\n153:         # monotonic decreasing\\n154:         engine = self.engine_type(arr[::-1])\\n155:         assert engine.is_monotonic_increasing is False\\n156:         assert engine.is_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n157: \\n158:         # neither monotonic increasing or decreasing\\n159:         arr = np.array([\\\"a\\\"] * num + [\\\"b\\\"] * num + [\\\"a\\\"] * num, dtype=self.dtype)\\n160:         engine = self.engine_type(arr[::-1])\\n161:         assert engine.is_monotonic_increasing is False\\n162:         assert engine.is_monotonic_decreasing is False\\n163: \\n164:     def test_is_unique(self):\\n165:         # unique\\n166:         arr = np.array(self.values, dtype=self.dtype)\\n167:         engine = self.engine_type(arr)\\n168:         assert engine.is_unique is True\\n169: \\n170:         # not unique\\n171:         arr = np.array([\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"a\\\"], dtype=self.dtype)\\n172:         engine = self.engine_type(arr)\\n173:         assert engine.is_unique is False\\n174: \\n175:     def test_get_loc(self):\\n176:         # unique\\n177:         arr = np.array(self.values, dtype=self.dtype)\\n178:         engine = self.engine_type(arr)\\n179:         assert engine.get_loc(\\\"b\\\") == 1\\n180: \\n181:         # monotonic\\n182:         num = 1000\\n183:         arr = np.array([\\\"a\\\"] * num + [\\\"b\\\"] * num + [\\\"c\\\"] * num, dtype=self.dtype)\\n184:         engine = self.engine_type(arr)\\n185:         assert engine.get_loc(\\\"b\\\") == slice(1000, 2000)\\n186: \\n187:         # not monotonic\\n188:         arr = np.array(self.values * num, dtype=self.dtype)\\n189:         engine = self.engine_type(arr)\\n190:         expected = np.array([False, True, False] * num, dtype=bool)\\n191:         result = engine.get_loc(\\\"b\\\")\\n192:         assert (result == expected).all()\\n193: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pandas--v2.2.3@d6878b8b2a7811e59d667a6ea7414a3a3721a764/-/blob/pandas/tests/indexes/test_engines.py\"}"
33. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "pandas/tests/indexes/ranges/test_range.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pandas--v2.2.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: import numpy as np\\n2: import pytest\\n3: \\n4: from pandas.core.dtypes.common import ensure_platform_int\\n5: \\n6: import pandas as pd\\n7: from pandas import (\\n8:     Index,\\n9:     RangeIndex,\\n10: )\\n11: import pandas._testing as tm\\n12: \\n13: \\n14: class TestRangeIndex:\\n15:     @pytest.fixture\\n16:     def simple_index(self):\\n17:         return RangeIndex(start=0, stop=20, step=2)\\n18: \\n19:     def test_constructor_unwraps_index(self):\\n20:         result = RangeIndex(1, 3)\\n21:         expected = np.array([1, 2], dtype=np.int64)\\n22:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result._data, expected)\\n23: \\n24:     def test_can_hold_identifiers(self, simple_index):\\n25:         idx = simple_index\\n26:         key = idx[0]\\n27:         assert idx._can_hold_identifiers_and_holds_name(key) is False\\n28: \\n29:     def test_too_many_names(self, simple_index):\\n30:         index = simple_index\\n31:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\\\"^Length\\\"):\\n32:             index.names = [\\\"roger\\\", \\\"harold\\\"]\\n33: \\n34:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n35:         \\\"index, start, stop, step\\\",\\n36:         [\\n37:             (RangeIndex(5), 0, 5, 1),\\n38:             (RangeIndex(0, 5), 0, 5, 1),\\n39:             (RangeIndex(5, step=2), 0, 5, 2),\\n40:             (RangeIndex(1, 5, 2), 1, 5, 2),\\n41:         ],\\n42:     )\\n43:     def test_start_stop_step_attrs(self, index, start, stop, step):\\n44:         # GH 25710\\n45:         assert index.start == start\\n46:         assert index.stop == stop\\n47:         assert index.step == step\\n48: \\n49:     def test_copy(self):\\n50:         i = RangeIndex(5, name=\\\"Foo\\\")\\n51:         i_copy = i.copy()\\n52:         assert i_copy is not i\\n53:         assert i_copy.identical(i)\\n54:         assert i_copy._range == range(0, 5, 1)\\n55:         assert i_copy.name == \\\"Foo\\\"\\n56: \\n57:     def test_repr(self):\\n58:         i = RangeIndex(5, name=\\\"Foo\\\")\\n59:         result = repr(i)\\n60:         expected = \\\"RangeIndex(start=0, stop=5, step=1, name='Foo')\\\"\\n61:         assert result == expected\\n62: \\n63:         result = eval(result)\\n64:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, i, exact=True)\\n65: \\n66:         i = RangeIndex(5, 0, -1)\\n67:         result = repr(i)\\n68:         expected = \\\"RangeIndex(start=5, stop=0, step=-1)\\\"\\n69:         assert result == expected\\n70: \\n71:         result = eval(result)\\n72:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, i, exact=True)\\n73: \\n74:     def test_insert(self):\\n75:         idx = RangeIndex(5, name=\\\"Foo\\\")\\n76:         result = idx[1:4]\\n77: \\n78:         # test 0th element\\n79:         tm.assert_index_equal(idx[0:4], result.insert(0, idx[0]), exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n80: \\n81:         # GH 18295 (test missing)\\n82:         expected = Index([0, np.nan, 1, 2, 3, 4], dtype=np.float64)\\n83:         for na in [np.nan, None, pd.NA]:\\n84:             result = RangeIndex(5).insert(1, na)\\n85:             tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected)\\n86: \\n87:         result = RangeIndex(5).insert(1, pd.NaT)\\n88:         expected = Index([0, pd.NaT, 1, 2, 3, 4], dtype=object)\\n89:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected)\\n90: \\n91:     def test_insert_edges_preserves_rangeindex(self):\\n92:         idx = Index(range(4, 9, 2))\\n93: \\n94:         result = idx.insert(0, 2)\\n95:         expected = Index(range(2, 9, 2))\\n96:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n97: \\n98:         result = idx.insert(3, 10)\\n99:         expected = Index(range(4, 11, 2))\\n100:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n101: \\n102:     def test_insert_middle_preserves_rangeindex(self):\\n103:         # insert in the middle\\n104:         idx = Index(range(0, 3, 2))\\n105:         result = idx.insert(1, 1)\\n106:         expected = Index(range(3))\\n107:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n108: \\n109:         idx = idx * 2\\n110:         result = idx.insert(1, 2)\\n111:         expected = expected * 2\\n112:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n113: \\n114:     def test_delete(self):\\n115:         idx = RangeIndex(5, name=\\\"Foo\\\")\\n116:         expected = idx[1:]\\n117:         result = idx.delete(0)\\n118:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n119:         assert result.name == expected.name\\n120: \\n121:         expected = idx[:-1]\\n122:         result = idx.delete(-1)\\n123:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n124:         assert result.name == expected.name\\n125: \\n126:         msg = \\\"index 5 is out of bounds for axis 0 with size 5\\\"\\n127:         with pytest.raises((IndexError, ValueError), match=msg):\\n128:             # either depending on numpy version\\n129:             result = idx.delete(len(idx))\\n130: \\n131:     def test_delete_preserves_rangeindex(self):\\n132:         idx = Index(range(2), name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n133: \\n134:         result = idx.delete([1])\\n135:         expected = Index(range(1), name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n136:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n137: \\n138:         result = idx.delete(1)\\n139:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n140: \\n141:     def test_delete_preserves_rangeindex_middle(self):\\n142:         idx = Index(range(3), name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n143:         result = idx.delete(1)\\n144:         expected = idx[::2]\\n145:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n146: \\n147:         result = idx.delete(-2)\\n148:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n149: \\n150:     def test_delete_preserves_rangeindex_list_at_end(self):\\n151:         idx = RangeIndex(0, 6, 1)\\n152: \\n153:         loc = [2, 3, 4, 5]\\n154:         result = idx.delete(loc)\\n155:         expected = idx[:2]\\n156:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n157: \\n158:         result = idx.delete(loc[::-1])\\n159:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n160: \\n161:     def test_delete_preserves_rangeindex_list_middle(self):\\n162:         idx = RangeIndex(0, 6, 1)\\n163: \\n164:         loc = [1, 2, 3, 4]\\n165:         result = idx.delete(loc)\\n166:         expected = RangeIndex(0, 6, 5)\\n167:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n168: \\n169:         result = idx.delete(loc[::-1])\\n170:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n171: \\n172:     def test_delete_all_preserves_rangeindex(self):\\n173:         idx = RangeIndex(0, 6, 1)\\n174: \\n175:         loc = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]\\n176:         result = idx.delete(loc)\\n177:         expected = idx[:0]\\n178:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n179: \\n180:         result = idx.delete(loc[::-1])\\n181:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n182: \\n183:     def test_delete_not_preserving_rangeindex(self):\\n184:         idx = RangeIndex(0, 6, 1)\\n185: \\n186:         loc = [0, 3, 5]\\n187:         result = idx.delete(loc)\\n188:         expected = Index([1, 2, 4])\\n189:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n190: \\n191:         result = idx.delete(loc[::-1])\\n192:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n193: \\n194:     def test_view(self):\\n195:         i = RangeIndex(0, name=\\\"Foo\\\")\\n196:         i_view = i.view()\\n197:         assert i_view.name == \\\"Foo\\\"\\n198: \\n199:         i_view = i.view(\\\"i8\\\")\\n200:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(i.values, i_view)\\n201: \\n202:         msg = \\\"Passing a type in RangeIndex.view is deprecated\\\"\\n203:         with tm.assert_produces_warning(FutureWarning, match=msg):\\n204:             i_view = i.view(RangeIndex)\\n205:         tm.assert_index_equal(i, i_view)\\n206: \\n207:     def test_dtype(self, simple_index):\\n208:         index = simple_index\\n209:         assert index.dtype == np.int64\\n210: \\n211:     def test_cache(self):\\n212:         # GH 26565, GH26617, GH35432, GH53387\\n213:         # This test checks whether _cache has been set.\\n214:         # Calling RangeIndex._cache[\\\"_data\\\"] creates an int64 array of the same length\\n215:         # as the RangeIndex and stores it in _cache.\\n216:         idx = RangeIndex(0, 100, 10)\\n217: \\n218:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n219: \\n220:         repr(idx)\\n221:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n222: \\n223:         str(idx)\\n224:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n225: \\n226:         idx.get_loc(20)\\n227:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n228: \\n229:         90 in idx  # True\\n230:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n231: \\n232:         91 in idx  # False\\n233:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n234: \\n235:         idx.all()\\n236:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n237: \\n238:         idx.any()\\n239:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n240: \\n241:         for _ in idx:\\n242:             pass\\n243:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n244: \\n245:         msg = \\\"RangeIndex.format is deprecated\\\"\\n246:         with tm.assert_produces_warning(FutureWarning, match=msg):\\n247:             idx.format()\\n248:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n249: \\n250:         df = pd.DataFrame({\\\"a\\\": range(10)}, index=idx)\\n251: \\n252:         # df.__repr__ should not populate index cache\\n253:         str(df)\\n254:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n255: \\n256:         df.loc[50]\\n257:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n258: \\n259:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=\\\"51\\\"):\\n260:             df.loc[51]\\n261:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n262: \\n263:         df.loc[10:50]\\n264:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n265: \\n266:         df.iloc[5:10]\\n267:         assert idx._cache == {}\\n268: \\n269:         # after calling take, _cache may contain other keys, but not \\\"_data\\\"\\n270:         idx.take([3, 0, 1])\\n271:         assert \\\"_data\\\" not in idx._cache\\n272: \\n273:         df.loc[[50]]\\n274:         assert \\\"_data\\\" not in idx._cache\\n275: \\n276:         df.iloc[[5, 6, 7, 8, 9]]\\n277:         assert \\\"_data\\\" not in idx._cache\\n278: \\n279:         # idx._cache should contain a _data entry after call to idx._data\\n280:         idx._data\\n281:         assert isinstance(idx._data, np.ndarray)\\n282:         assert idx._data is idx._data  # check cached value is reused\\n283:         assert \\\"_data\\\" in idx._cache\\n284:         expected = np.arange(0, 100, 10, dtype=\\\"int64\\\")\\n285:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(idx._cache[\\\"_data\\\"], expected)\\n286: \\n287:     def test_is_monotonic(self):\\n288:         index = RangeIndex(0, 20, 2)\\n289:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n290:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n291:         assert index.is_monotonic_decreasing is False\\n292:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_increasing is True\\n293:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_decreasing is False\\n294: \\n295:         index = RangeIndex(4, 0, -1)\\n296:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is False\\n297:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_increasing is False\\n298:         assert index.is_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n299:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n300: \\n301:         index = RangeIndex(1, 2)\\n302:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n303:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n304:         assert index.is_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n305:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_increasing is True\\n306:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n307: \\n308:         index = RangeIndex(2, 1)\\n309:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n310:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n311:         assert index.is_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n312:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_increasing is True\\n313:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n314: \\n315:         index = RangeIndex(1, 1)\\n316:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n317:         assert index.is_monotonic_increasing is True\\n318:         assert index.is_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n319:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_increasing is True\\n320:         assert index._is_strictly_monotonic_decreasing is True\\n321: \\n322:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n323:         \\\"left,right\\\",\\n324:         [\\n325:             (RangeIndex(0, 9, 2), RangeIndex(0, 10, 2)),\\n326:             (RangeIndex(0), RangeIndex(1, -1, 3)),\\n327:             (RangeIndex(1, 2, 3), RangeIndex(1, 3, 4)),\\n328:             (RangeIndex(0, -9, -2), RangeIndex(0, -10, -2)),\\n329:         ],\\n330:     )\\n331:     def test_equals_range(self, left, right):\\n332:         assert left.equals(right)\\n333:         assert right.equals(left)\\n334: \\n335:     def test_logical_compat(self, simple_index):\\n336:         idx = simple_index\\n337:         assert idx.all() == idx.values.all()\\n338:         assert idx.any() == idx.values.any()\\n339: \\n340:     def test_identical(self, simple_index):\\n341:         index = simple_index\\n342:         i = Index(index.copy())\\n343:         assert i.identical(index)\\n344: \\n345:         # we don't allow object dtype for RangeIndex\\n346:         if isinstance(index, RangeIndex):\\n347:             return\\n348: \\n349:         same_values_different_type = Index(i, dtype=object)\\n350:         assert not i.identical(same_values_different_type)\\n351: \\n352:         i = index.copy(dtype=object)\\n353:         i = i.rename(\\\"foo\\\")\\n354:         same_values = Index(i, dtype=object)\\n355:         assert same_values.identical(index.copy(dtype=object))\\n356: \\n357:         assert not i.identical(index)\\n358:         assert Index(same_values, name=\\\"foo\\\", dtype=object).identical(i)\\n359: \\n360:         assert not index.copy(dtype=object).identical(index.copy(dtype=\\\"int64\\\"))\\n361: \\n362:     def test_nbytes(self):\\n363:         # memory savings vs int index\\n364:         idx = RangeIndex(0, 1000)\\n365:         assert idx.nbytes < Index(idx._values).nbytes / 10\\n366: \\n367:         # constant memory usage\\n368:         i2 = RangeIndex(0, 10)\\n369:         assert idx.nbytes == i2.nbytes\\n370: \\n371:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n372:         \\\"start,stop,step\\\",\\n373:         [\\n374:             # can't\\n375:             (\\\"foo\\\", \\\"bar\\\", \\\"baz\\\"),\\n376:             # shouldn't\\n377:             (\\\"0\\\", \\\"1\\\", \\\"2\\\"),\\n378:         ],\\n379:     )\\n380:     def test_cant_or_shouldnt_cast(self, start, stop, step):\\n381:         msg = f\\\"Wrong type {type(start)} for value {start}\\\"\\n382:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=msg):\\n383:             RangeIndex(start, stop, step)\\n384: \\n385:     def test_view_index(self, simple_index):\\n386:         index = simple_index\\n387:         msg = \\\"Passing a type in RangeIndex.view is deprecated\\\"\\n388:         with tm.assert_produces_warning(FutureWarning, match=msg):\\n389:             index.view(Index)\\n390: \\n391:     def test_prevent_casting(self, simple_index):\\n392:         index = simple_index\\n393:         result = index.astype(\\\"O\\\")\\n394:         assert result.dtype == np.object_\\n395: \\n396:     def test_repr_roundtrip(self, simple_index):\\n397:         index = simple_index\\n398:         tm.assert_index_equal(eval(repr(index)), index)\\n399: \\n400:     def test_slice_keep_name(self):\\n401:         idx = RangeIndex(1, 2, name=\\\"asdf\\\")\\n402:         assert idx.name == idx[1:].name\\n403: \\n404:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n405:         \\\"index\\\",\\n406:         [\\n407:             RangeIndex(start=0, stop=20, step=2, name=\\\"foo\\\"),\\n408:             RangeIndex(start=18, stop=-1, step=-2, name=\\\"bar\\\"),\\n409:         ],\\n410:         ids=[\\\"index_inc\\\", \\\"index_dec\\\"],\\n411:     )\\n412:     def test_has_duplicates(self, index):\\n413:         assert index.is_unique\\n414:         assert not index.has_duplicates\\n415: \\n416:     def test_extended_gcd(self, simple_index):\\n417:         index = simple_index\\n418:         result = index._extended_gcd(6, 10)\\n419:         assert result[0] == result[1] * 6 + result[2] * 10\\n420:         assert 2 == result[0]\\n421: \\n422:         result = index._extended_gcd(10, 6)\\n423:         assert 2 == result[1] * 10 + result[2] * 6\\n424:         assert 2 == result[0]\\n425: \\n426:     def test_min_fitting_element(self):\\n427:         result = RangeIndex(0, 20, 2)._min_fitting_element(1)\\n428:         assert 2 == result\\n429: \\n430:         result = RangeIndex(1, 6)._min_fitting_element(1)\\n431:         assert 1 == result\\n432: \\n433:         result = RangeIndex(18, -2, -2)._min_fitting_element(1)\\n434:         assert 2 == result\\n435: \\n436:         result = RangeIndex(5, 0, -1)._min_fitting_element(1)\\n437:         assert 1 == result\\n438: \\n439:         big_num = 500000000000000000000000\\n440: \\n441:         result = RangeIndex(5, big_num * 2, 1)._min_fitting_element(big_num)\\n442:         assert big_num == result\\n443: \\n444:     def test_slice_specialised(self, simple_index):\\n445:         index = simple_index\\n446:         index.name = \\\"foo\\\"\\n447: \\n448:         # scalar indexing\\n449:         res = index[1]\\n450:         expected = 2\\n451:         assert res == expected\\n452: \\n453:         res = index[-1]\\n454:         expected = 18\\n455:         assert res == expected\\n456: \\n457:         # slicing\\n458:         # slice value completion\\n459:         index_slice = index[:]\\n460:         expected = index\\n461:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected)\\n462: \\n463:         # positive slice values\\n464:         index_slice = index[7:10:2]\\n465:         expected = Index([14, 18], name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n466:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected, exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n467: \\n468:         # negative slice values\\n469:         index_slice = index[-1:-5:-2]\\n470:         expected = Index([18, 14], name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n471:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected, exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n472: \\n473:         # stop overshoot\\n474:         index_slice = index[2:100:4]\\n475:         expected = Index([4, 12], name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n476:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected, exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n477: \\n478:         # reverse\\n479:         index_slice = index[::-1]\\n480:         expected = Index(index.values[::-1], name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n481:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected, exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n482: \\n483:         index_slice = index[-8::-1]\\n484:         expected = Index([4, 2, 0], name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n485:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected, exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n486: \\n487:         index_slice = index[-40::-1]\\n488:         expected = Index(np.array([], dtype=np.int64), name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n489:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected, exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n490: \\n491:         index_slice = index[40::-1]\\n492:         expected = Index(index.values[40::-1], name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n493:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected, exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n494: \\n495:         index_slice = index[10::-1]\\n496:         expected = Index(index.values[::-1], name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n497:         tm.assert_index_equal(index_slice, expected, exact=\\\"equiv\\\")\\n498: \\n499:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"step\\\", set(range(-5, 6)) - {0})\\n500:     def test_len_specialised(self, step):\\n501:         # make sure that our len is the same as np.arange calc\\n502:         start, stop = (0, 5) if step > 0 else (5, 0)\\n503: \\n504:         arr = np.arange(start, stop, step)\\n505:         index = RangeIndex(start, stop, step)\\n506:         assert len(index) == len(arr)\\n507: \\n508:         index = RangeIndex(stop, start, step)\\n509:         assert len(index) == 0\\n510: \\n511:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n512:         \\\"indices, expected\\\",\\n513:         [\\n514:             ([RangeIndex(1, 12, 5)], RangeIndex(1, 12, 5)),\\n515:             ([RangeIndex(0, 6, 4)], RangeIndex(0, 6, 4)),\\n516:             ([RangeIndex(1, 3), RangeIndex(3, 7)], RangeIndex(1, 7)),\\n517:             ([RangeIndex(1, 5, 2), RangeIndex(5, 6)], RangeIndex(1, 6, 2)),\\n518:             ([RangeIndex(1, 3, 2), RangeIndex(4, 7, 3)], RangeIndex(1, 7, 3)),\\n519:             ([RangeIndex(-4, 3, 2), RangeIndex(4, 7, 2)], RangeIndex(-4, 7, 2)),\\n520:             ([RangeIndex(-4, -8), RangeIndex(-8, -12)], RangeIndex(0, 0)),\\n521:             ([RangeIndex(-4, -8), RangeIndex(3, -4)], RangeIndex(0, 0)),\\n522:             ([RangeIndex(-4, -8), RangeIndex(3, 5)], RangeIndex(3, 5)),\\n523:             ([RangeIndex(-4, -2), RangeIndex(3, 5)], Index([-4, -3, 3, 4])),\\n524:             ([RangeIndex(-2), RangeIndex(3, 5)], RangeIndex(3, 5)),\\n525:             ([RangeIndex(2), RangeIndex(2)], Index([0, 1, 0, 1])),\\n526:             ([RangeIndex(2), RangeIndex(2, 5), RangeIndex(5, 8, 4)], RangeIndex(0, 6)),\\n527:             (\\n528:                 [RangeIndex(2), RangeIndex(3, 5), RangeIndex(5, 8, 4)],\\n529:                 Index([0, 1, 3, 4, 5]),\\n530:             ),\\n531:             (\\n532:                 [RangeIndex(-2, 2), RangeIndex(2, 5), RangeIndex(5, 8, 4)],\\n533:                 RangeIndex(-2, 6),\\n534:             ),\\n535:             ([RangeIndex(3), Index([-1, 3, 15])], Index([0, 1, 2, -1, 3, 15])),\\n536:             ([RangeIndex(3), Index([-1, 3.1, 15.0])], Index([0, 1, 2, -1, 3.1, 15.0])),\\n537:             ([RangeIndex(3), Index([\\\"a\\\", None, 14])], Index([0, 1, 2, \\\"a\\\", None, 14])),\\n538:             ([RangeIndex(3, 1), Index([\\\"a\\\", None, 14])], Index([\\\"a\\\", None, 14])),\\n539:         ],\\n540:     )\\n541:     def test_append(self, indices, expected):\\n542:         # GH16212\\n543:         result = indices[0].append(indices[1:])\\n544:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, exact=True)\\n545: \\n546:         if len(indices) == 2:\\n547:             # Append single item rather than list\\n548:             result2 = indices[0].append(indices[1])\\n549:             tm.assert_index_equal(result2, expected, exact=True)\\n550: \\n551:     def test_engineless_lookup(self):\\n552:         # GH 16685\\n553:         # Standard lookup on RangeIndex should not require the engine to be\\n554:         # created\\n555:         idx = RangeIndex(2, 10, 3)\\n556: \\n557:         assert idx.get_loc(5) == 1\\n558:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(\\n559:             idx.get_indexer([2, 8]), ensure_platform_int(np.array([0, 2]))\\n560:         )\\n561:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=\\\"3\\\"):\\n562:             idx.get_loc(3)\\n563: \\n564:         assert \\\"_engine\\\" not in idx._cache\\n565: \\n566:         # Different types of scalars can be excluded immediately, no need to\\n567:         #  use the _engine\\n568:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=\\\"'a'\\\"):\\n569:             idx.get_loc(\\\"a\\\")\\n570: \\n571:         assert \\\"_engine\\\" not in idx._cache\\n572: \\n573:     def test_format_empty(self):\\n574:         # GH35712\\n575:         empty_idx = RangeIndex(0)\\n576:         msg = r\\\"RangeIndex\\\\.format is deprecated\\\"\\n577:         with tm.assert_produces_warning(FutureWarning, match=msg):\\n578:             assert empty_idx.format() == []\\n579:         with tm.assert_produces_warning(FutureWarning, match=msg):\\n580:             assert empty_idx.format(name=True) == [\\\"\\\"]\\n581: \\n582:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n583:         \\\"ri\\\",\\n584:         [\\n585:             RangeIndex(0, -1, -1),\\n586:             RangeIndex(0, 1, 1),\\n587:             RangeIndex(1, 3, 2),\\n588:             RangeIndex(0, -1, -2),\\n589:             RangeIndex(-3, -5, -2),\\n590:         ],\\n591:     )\\n592:     def test_append_len_one(self, ri):\\n593:         # GH39401\\n594:         result = ri.append([])\\n595:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, ri, exact=True)\\n596: \\n597:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"base\\\", [RangeIndex(0, 2), Index([0, 1])])\\n598:     def test_isin_range(self, base):\\n599:         # GH#41151\\n600:         values = RangeIndex(0, 1)\\n601:         result = base.isin(values)\\n602:         expected = np.array([True, False])\\n603:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected)\\n604: \\n605:     def test_sort_values_key(self):\\n606:         # GH#43666, GH#52764\\n607:         sort_order = {8: 2, 6: 0, 4: 8, 2: 10, 0: 12}\\n608:         values = RangeIndex(0, 10, 2)\\n609:         result = values.sort_values(key=lambda x: x.map(sort_order))\\n610:         expected = Index([6, 8, 4, 2, 0], dtype=\\\"int64\\\")\\n611:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected, check_exact=True)\\n612: \\n613:         # check this matches the Series.sort_values behavior\\n614:         ser = values.to_series()\\n615:         result2 = ser.sort_values(key=lambda x: x.map(sort_order))\\n616:         tm.assert_series_equal(result2, expected.to_series(), check_exact=True)\\n617: \\n618:     def test_range_index_rsub_by_const(self):\\n619:         # GH#53255\\n620:         result = 3 - RangeIndex(0, 4, 1)\\n621:         expected = RangeIndex(3, -1, -1)\\n622:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected)\\n623: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pandas--v2.2.3@d6878b8b2a7811e59d667a6ea7414a3a3721a764/-/blob/pandas/tests/indexes/ranges/test_range.py\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: from datetime import timedelta\\n2: import re\\n3: \\n4: import numpy as np\\n5: import pytest\\n6: \\n7: from pandas._libs import index as libindex\\n8: from pandas.errors import (\\n9:     InvalidIndexError,\\n10:     PerformanceWarning,\\n11: )\\n12: \\n13: import pandas as pd\\n14: from pandas import (\\n15:     Categorical,\\n16:     DataFrame,\\n17:     Index,\\n18:     MultiIndex,\\n19:     date_range,\\n20: )\\n21: import pandas._testing as tm\\n22: \\n23: \\n24: class TestSliceLocs:\\n25:     def test_slice_locs_partial(self, idx):\\n26:         sorted_idx, _ = idx.sortlevel(0)\\n27: \\n28:         result = sorted_idx.slice_locs((\\\"foo\\\", \\\"two\\\"), (\\\"qux\\\", \\\"one\\\"))\\n29:         assert result == (1, 5)\\n30: \\n31:         result = sorted_idx.slice_locs(None, (\\\"qux\\\", \\\"one\\\"))\\n32:         assert result == (0, 5)\\n33: \\n34:         result = sorted_idx.slice_locs((\\\"foo\\\", \\\"two\\\"), None)\\n35:         assert result == (1, len(sorted_idx))\\n36: \\n37:         result = sorted_idx.slice_locs(\\\"bar\\\", \\\"baz\\\")\\n38:         assert result == (2, 4)\\n39: \\n40:     def test_slice_locs(self):\\n41:         df = DataFrame(\\n42:             np.random.default_rng(2).standard_normal((50, 4)),\\n43:             columns=Index(list(\\\"ABCD\\\"), dtype=object),\\n44:             index=date_range(\\\"2000-01-01\\\", periods=50, freq=\\\"B\\\"),\\n45:         )\\n46:         stacked = df.stack(future_stack=True)\\n47:         idx = stacked.index\\n48: \\n49:         slob = slice(*idx.slice_locs(df.index[5], df.index[15]))\\n50:         sliced = stacked[slob]\\n51:         expected = df[5:16].stack(future_stack=True)\\n52:         tm.assert_almost_equal(sliced.values, expected.values)\\n53: \\n54:         slob = slice(\\n55:             *idx.slice_locs(\\n56:                 df.index[5] + timedelta(seconds=30),\\n57:                 df.index[15] - timedelta(seconds=30),\\n58:             )\\n59:         )\\n60:         sliced = stacked[slob]\\n61:         expected = df[6:15].stack(future_stack=True)\\n62:         tm.assert_almost_equal(sliced.values, expected.values)\\n63: \\n64:     def test_slice_locs_with_type_mismatch(self):\\n65:         df = DataFrame(\\n66:             np.random.default_rng(2).standard_normal((10, 4)),\\n67:             columns=Index(list(\\\"ABCD\\\"), dtype=object),\\n68:             index=date_range(\\\"2000-01-01\\\", periods=10, freq=\\\"B\\\"),\\n69:         )\\n70:         stacked = df.stack(future_stack=True)\\n71:         idx = stacked.index\\n72:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\\\"^Level type mismatch\\\"):\\n73:             idx.slice_locs((1, 3))\\n74:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\\\"^Level type mismatch\\\"):\\n75:             idx.slice_locs(df.index[5] + timedelta(seconds=30), (5, 2))\\n76:         df = DataFrame(\\n77:             np.ones((5, 5)),\\n78:             index=Index([f\\\"i-{i}\\\" for i in range(5)], name=\\\"a\\\"),\\n79:             columns=Index([f\\\"i-{i}\\\" for i in range(5)], name=\\\"a\\\"),\\n80:         )\\n81:         stacked = df.stack(future_stack=True)\\n82:         idx = stacked.index\\n83:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\\\"^Level type mismatch\\\"):\\n84:             idx.slice_locs(timedelta(seconds=30))\\n85:         # TODO: Try creating a UnicodeDecodeError in exception message\\n86:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\\\"^Level type mismatch\\\"):\\n87:             idx.slice_locs(df.index[1], (16, \\\"a\\\"))\\n88: \\n89:     def test_slice_locs_not_sorted(self):\\n90:         index = MultiIndex(\\n91:             levels=[Index(np.arange(4)), Index(np.arange(4)), Index(np.arange(4))],\\n92:             codes=[\\n93:                 np.array([0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3]),\\n94:                 np.array([0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1]),\\n95:                 np.array([1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0]),\\n96:             ],\\n97:         )\\n98:         msg = \\\"[Kk]ey length.*greater than MultiIndex lexsort depth\\\"\\n99:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=msg):\\n100:             index.slice_locs((1, 0, 1), (2, 1, 0))\\n101: \\n102:         # works\\n103:         sorted_index, _ = index.sortlevel(0)\\n104:         # should there be a test case here???\\n105:         sorted_index.slice_locs((1, 0, 1), (2, 1, 0))\\n106: \\n107:     def test_slice_locs_not_contained(self):\\n108:         # some searchsorted action\\n109: \\n110:         index = MultiIndex(\\n111:             levels=[[0, 2, 4, 6], [0, 2, 4]],\\n112:             codes=[[0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 3, 3], [0, 1, 2, 1, 2, 2, 0, 1, 2]],\\n113:         )\\n114: \\n115:         result = index.slice_locs((1, 0), (5, 2))\\n116:         assert result == (3, 6)\\n117: \\n118:         result = index.slice_locs(1, 5)\\n119:         assert result == (3, 6)\\n120: \\n121:         result = index.slice_locs((2, 2), (5, 2))\\n122:         assert result == (3, 6)\\n123: \\n124:         result = index.slice_locs(2, 5)\\n125:         assert result == (3, 6)\\n126: \\n127:         result = index.slice_locs((1, 0), (6, 3))\\n128:         assert result == (3, 8)\\n129: \\n130:         result = index.slice_locs(-1, 10)\\n131:         assert result == (0, len(index))\\n132: \\n133:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n134:         \\\"index_arr,expected,start_idx,end_idx\\\",\\n135:         [\\n136:             ([[np.nan, \\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\"], [\\\"c\\\", \\\"d\\\", \\\"e\\\"]], (0, 3), np.nan, None),\\n137:             ([[np.nan, \\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\"], [\\\"c\\\", \\\"d\\\", \\\"e\\\"]], (0, 3), np.nan, \\\"b\\\"),\\n138:             ([[np.nan, \\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\"], [\\\"c\\\", \\\"d\\\", \\\"e\\\"]], (0, 3), np.nan, (\\\"b\\\", \\\"e\\\")),\\n139:             ([[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"], [\\\"d\\\", np.nan, \\\"e\\\"]], (1, 3), (\\\"b\\\", np.nan), None),\\n140:             ([[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"], [\\\"d\\\", np.nan, \\\"e\\\"]], (1, 3), (\\\"b\\\", np.nan), \\\"c\\\"),\\n141:             ([[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"], [\\\"d\\\", np.nan, \\\"e\\\"]], (1, 3), (\\\"b\\\", np.nan), (\\\"c\\\", \\\"e\\\")),\\n142:         ],\\n143:     )\\n144:     def test_slice_locs_with_missing_value(\\n145:         self, index_arr, expected, start_idx, end_idx\\n146:     ):\\n147:         # issue 19132\\n148:         idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays(index_arr)\\n149:         result = idx.slice_locs(start=start_idx, end=end_idx)\\n150:         assert result == expected\\n151: \\n152: \\n153: class TestPutmask:\\n154:     def test_putmask_with_wrong_mask(self, idx):\\n155:         # GH18368\\n156: \\n157:         msg = \\\"putmask: mask and data must be the same size\\\"\\n158:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\\n159:             idx.putmask(np.ones(len(idx) + 1, np.bool_), 1)\\n160: \\n161:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\\n162:             idx.putmask(np.ones(len(idx) - 1, np.bool_), 1)\\n163: \\n164:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\\n165:             idx.putmask(\\\"foo\\\", 1)\\n166: \\n167:     def test_putmask_multiindex_other(self):\\n168:         # GH#43212 `value` is also a MultiIndex\\n169: \\n170:         left = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(np.nan, 6), (np.nan, 6), (\\\"a\\\", 4)])\\n171:         right = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(\\\"a\\\", 1), (\\\"a\\\", 1), (\\\"d\\\", 1)])\\n172:         mask = np.array([True, True, False])\\n173: \\n174:         result = left.putmask(mask, right)\\n175: \\n176:         expected = MultiIndex.from_tuples([right[0], right[1], left[2]])\\n177:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected)\\n178: \\n179:     def test_putmask_keep_dtype(self, any_numeric_ea_dtype):\\n180:         # GH#49830\\n181:         midx = MultiIndex.from_arrays(\\n182:             [pd.Series([1, 2, 3], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype), [10, 11, 12]]\\n183:         )\\n184:         midx2 = MultiIndex.from_arrays(\\n185:             [pd.Series([5, 6, 7], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype), [-1, -2, -3]]\\n186:         )\\n187:         result = midx.putmask([True, False, False], midx2)\\n188:         expected = MultiIndex.from_arrays(\\n189:             [pd.Series([5, 2, 3], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype), [-1, 11, 12]]\\n190:         )\\n191:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected)\\n192: \\n193:     def test_putmask_keep_dtype_shorter_value(self, any_numeric_ea_dtype):\\n194:         # GH#49830\\n195:         midx = MultiIndex.from_arrays(\\n196:             [pd.Series([1, 2, 3], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype), [10, 11, 12]]\\n197:         )\\n198:         midx2 = MultiIndex.from_arrays(\\n199:             [pd.Series([5], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype), [-1]]\\n200:         )\\n201:         result = midx.putmask([True, False, False], midx2)\\n202:         expected = MultiIndex.from_arrays(\\n203:             [pd.Series([5, 2, 3], dtype=any_numeric_ea_dtype), [-1, 11, 12]]\\n204:         )\\n205:         tm.assert_index_equal(result, expected)\\n206: \\n207: \\n208: class TestGetIndexer:\\n209:     def test_get_indexer(self):\\n210:         major_axis = Index(np.arange(4))\\n211:         minor_axis = Index(np.arange(2))\\n212: \\n213:         major_codes = np.array([0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3], dtype=np.intp)\\n214:         minor_codes = np.array([0, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1], dtype=np.intp)\\n215: \\n216:         index = MultiIndex(\\n217:             levels=[major_axis, minor_axis], codes=[major_codes, minor_codes]\\n218:         )\\n219:         idx1 = index[:5]\\n220:         idx2 = index[[1, 3, 5]]\\n221: \\n222:         r1 = idx1.get_indexer(idx2)\\n223:         tm.assert_almost_equal(r1, np.array([1, 3, -1], dtype=np.intp))\\n224: \\n225:         r1 = idx2.get_indexer(idx1, method=\\\"pad\\\")\\n226:         e1 = np.array([-1, 0, 0, 1, 1], dtype=np.intp)\\n227:         tm.assert_almost_equal(r1, e1)\\n228: \\n229:         r2 = idx2.get_indexer(idx1[::-1], method=\\\"pad\\\")\\n230:         tm.assert_almost_equal(r2, e1[::-1])\\n231: \\n232:         rffill1 = idx2.get_indexer(idx1, method=\\\"ffill\\\")\\n233:         tm.assert_almost_equal(r1, rffill1)\\n234: \\n235:         r1 = idx2.get_indexer(idx1, method=\\\"backfill\\\")\\n236:         e1 = np.array([0, 0, 1, 1, 2], dtype=np.intp)\\n237:         tm.assert_almost_equal(r1, e1)\\n238: \\n239:         r2 = idx2.get_indexer(idx1[::-1], method=\\\"backfill\\\")\\n240:         tm.assert_almost_equal(r2, e1[::-1])\\n241: \\n242:         rbfill1 = idx2.get_indexer(idx1, method=\\\"bfill\\\")\\n243:         tm.assert_almost_equal(r1, rbfill1)\\n244: \\n245:         # pass non-MultiIndex\\n246:         r1 = idx1.get_indexer(idx2.values)\\n247:         rexp1 = idx1.get_indexer(idx2)\\n248:         tm.assert_almost_equal(r1, rexp1)\\n249: \\n250:         r1 = idx1.get_indexer([1, 2, 3])\\n251:         assert (r1 == [-1, -1, -1]).all()\\n252: \\n253:         # create index with duplicates\\n254:         idx1 = Index(list(range(10)) + list(range(10)))\\n255:         idx2 = Index(list(range(20)))\\n256: \\n257:         msg = \\\"Reindexing only valid with uniquely valued Index objects\\\"\\n258:         with pytest.raises(InvalidIndexError, match=msg):\\n259:             idx1.get_indexer(idx2)\\n260: \\n261:     def test_get_indexer_nearest(self):\\n262:         midx = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(\\\"a\\\", 1), (\\\"b\\\", 2)])\\n263:         msg = (\\n264:             \\\"method='nearest' not implemented yet for MultiIndex; \\\"\\n265:             \\\"see GitHub issue 9365\\\"\\n266:         )\\n267:         with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError, match=msg):\\n268:             midx.get_indexer([\\\"a\\\"], method=\\\"nearest\\\")\\n269:         msg = \\\"tolerance not implemented yet for MultiIndex\\\"\\n270:         with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError, match=msg):\\n271:             midx.get_indexer([\\\"a\\\"], method=\\\"pad\\\", tolerance=2)\\n272: \\n273:     def test_get_indexer_categorical_time(self):\\n274:         # https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/21390\\n275:         midx = MultiIndex.from_product(\\n276:             [\\n277:                 Categorical([\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"]),\\n278:                 Categorical(date_range(\\\"2012-01-01\\\", periods=3, freq=\\\"h\\\")),\\n279:             ]\\n280:         )\\n281:         result = midx.get_indexer(midx)\\n282:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, np.arange(9, dtype=np.intp))\\n283: \\n284:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n285:         \\\"index_arr,labels,expected\\\",\\n286:         [\\n287:             (\\n288:                 [[1, np.nan, 2], [3, 4, 5]],\\n289:                 [1, np.nan, 2],\\n290:                 np.array([-1, -1, -1], dtype=np.intp),\\n291:             ),\\n292:             ([[1, np.nan, 2], [3, 4, 5]], [(np.nan, 4)], np.array([1], dtype=np.intp)),\\n293:             ([[1, 2, 3], [np.nan, 4, 5]], [(1, np.nan)], np.array([0], dtype=np.intp)),\\n294:             (\\n295:                 [[1, 2, 3], [np.nan, 4, 5]],\\n296:                 [np.nan, 4, 5],\\n297:                 np.array([-1, -1, -1], dtype=np.intp),\\n298:             ),\\n299:         ],\\n300:     )\\n301:     def test_get_indexer_with_missing_value(self, index_arr, labels, expected):\\n302:         # issue 19132\\n303:         idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays(index_arr)\\n304:         result = idx.get_indexer(labels)\\n305:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected)\\n306: \\n307:     def test_get_indexer_methods(self):\\n308:         # https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/29896\\n309:         # test getting an indexer for another index with different methods\\n310:         # confirms that getting an indexer without a filling method, getting an\\n311:         # indexer and backfilling, and getting an indexer and padding all behave\\n312:         # correctly in the case where all of the target values fall in between\\n313:         # several levels in the MultiIndex into which they are getting an indexer\\n314:         #\\n315:         # visually, the MultiIndexes used in this test are:\\n316:         # mult_idx_1:\\n317:         #  0: -1 0\\n318:         #  1:    2\\n319:         #  2:    3\\n320:         #  3:    4\\n321:         #  4:  0 0\\n322:         #  5:    2\\n323:         #  6:    3\\n324:         #  7:    4\\n325:         #  8:  1 0\\n326:         #  9:    2\\n327:         # 10:    3\\n328:         # 11:    4\\n329:         #\\n330:         # mult_idx_2:\\n331:         #  0: 0 1\\n332:         #  1:   3\\n333:         #  2:   4\\n334:         mult_idx_1 = MultiIndex.from_product([[-1, 0, 1], [0, 2, 3, 4]])\\n335:         mult_idx_2 = MultiIndex.from_product([[0], [1, 3, 4]])\\n336: \\n337:         indexer = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2)\\n338:         expected = np.array([-1, 6, 7], dtype=indexer.dtype)\\n339:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, indexer)\\n340: \\n341:         backfill_indexer = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2, method=\\\"backfill\\\")\\n342:         expected = np.array([5, 6, 7], dtype=backfill_indexer.dtype)\\n343:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, backfill_indexer)\\n344: \\n345:         # ensure the legacy \\\"bfill\\\" option functions identically to \\\"backfill\\\"\\n346:         backfill_indexer = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2, method=\\\"bfill\\\")\\n347:         expected = np.array([5, 6, 7], dtype=backfill_indexer.dtype)\\n348:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, backfill_indexer)\\n349: \\n350:         pad_indexer = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2, method=\\\"pad\\\")\\n351:         expected = np.array([4, 6, 7], dtype=pad_indexer.dtype)\\n352:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, pad_indexer)\\n353: \\n354:         # ensure the legacy \\\"ffill\\\" option functions identically to \\\"pad\\\"\\n355:         pad_indexer = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2, method=\\\"ffill\\\")\\n356:         expected = np.array([4, 6, 7], dtype=pad_indexer.dtype)\\n357:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, pad_indexer)\\n358: \\n359:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"method\\\", [\\\"pad\\\", \\\"ffill\\\", \\\"backfill\\\", \\\"bfill\\\", \\\"nearest\\\"])\\n360:     def test_get_indexer_methods_raise_for_non_monotonic(self, method):\\n361:         # 53452\\n362:         mi = MultiIndex.from_arrays([[0, 4, 2], [0, 4, 2]])\\n363:         if method == \\\"nearest\\\":\\n364:             err = NotImplementedError\\n365:             msg = \\\"not implemented yet for MultiIndex\\\"\\n366:         else:\\n367:             err = ValueError\\n368:             msg = \\\"index must be monotonic increasing or decreasing\\\"\\n369:         with pytest.raises(err, match=msg):\\n370:             mi.get_indexer([(1, 1)], method=method)\\n371: \\n372:     def test_get_indexer_three_or_more_levels(self):\\n373:         # https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/29896\\n374:         # tests get_indexer() on MultiIndexes with 3+ levels\\n375:         # visually, these are\\n376:         # mult_idx_1:\\n377:         #  0: 1 2 5\\n378:         #  1:     7\\n379:         #  2:   4 5\\n380:         #  3:     7\\n381:         #  4:   6 5\\n382:         #  5:     7\\n383:         #  6: 3 2 5\\n384:         #  7:     7\\n385:         #  8:   4 5\\n386:         #  9:     7\\n387:         # 10:   6 5\\n388:         # 11:     7\\n389:         #\\n390:         # mult_idx_2:\\n391:         #  0: 1 1 8\\n392:         #  1: 1 5 9\\n393:         #  2: 1 6 7\\n394:         #  3: 2 1 6\\n395:         #  4: 2 7 6\\n396:         #  5: 2 7 8\\n397:         #  6: 3 6 8\\n398:         mult_idx_1 = MultiIndex.from_product([[1, 3], [2, 4, 6], [5, 7]])\\n399:         mult_idx_2 = MultiIndex.from_tuples(\\n400:             [\\n401:                 (1, 1, 8),\\n402:                 (1, 5, 9),\\n403:                 (1, 6, 7),\\n404:                 (2, 1, 6),\\n405:                 (2, 7, 7),\\n406:                 (2, 7, 8),\\n407:                 (3, 6, 8),\\n408:             ]\\n409:         )\\n410:         # sanity check\\n411:         assert mult_idx_1.is_monotonic_increasing\\n412:         assert mult_idx_1.is_unique\\n413:         assert mult_idx_2.is_monotonic_increasing\\n414:         assert mult_idx_2.is_unique\\n415: \\n416:         # show the relationships between the two\\n417:         assert mult_idx_2[0] < mult_idx_1[0]\\n418:         assert mult_idx_1[3] < mult_idx_2[1] < mult_idx_1[4]\\n419:         assert mult_idx_1[5] == mult_idx_2[2]\\n420:         assert mult_idx_1[5] < mult_idx_2[3] < mult_idx_1[6]\\n421:         assert mult_idx_1[5] < mult_idx_2[4] < mult_idx_1[6]\\n422:         assert mult_idx_1[5] < mult_idx_2[5] < mult_idx_1[6]\\n423:         assert mult_idx_1[-1] < mult_idx_2[6]\\n424: \\n425:         indexer_no_fill = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2)\\n426:         expected = np.array([-1, -1, 5, -1, -1, -1, -1], dtype=indexer_no_fill.dtype)\\n427:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, indexer_no_fill)\\n428: \\n429:         # test with backfilling\\n430:         indexer_backfilled = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2, method=\\\"backfill\\\")\\n431:         expected = np.array([0, 4, 5, 6, 6, 6, -1], dtype=indexer_backfilled.dtype)\\n432:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, indexer_backfilled)\\n433: \\n434:         # now, the same thing, but forward-filled (aka \\\"padded\\\")\\n435:         indexer_padded = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2, method=\\\"pad\\\")\\n436:         expected = np.array([-1, 3, 5, 5, 5, 5, 11], dtype=indexer_padded.dtype)\\n437:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, indexer_padded)\\n438: \\n439:         # now, do the indexing in the other direction\\n440:         assert mult_idx_2[0] < mult_idx_1[0] < mult_idx_2[1]\\n441:         assert mult_idx_2[0] < mult_idx_1[1] < mult_idx_2[1]\\n442:         assert mult_idx_2[0] < mult_idx_1[2] < mult_idx_2[1]\\n443:         assert mult_idx_2[0] < mult_idx_1[3] < mult_idx_2[1]\\n444:         assert mult_idx_2[1] < mult_idx_1[4] < mult_idx_2[2]\\n445:         assert mult_idx_2[2] == mult_idx_1[5]\\n446:         assert mult_idx_2[5] < mult_idx_1[6] < mult_idx_2[6]\\n447:         assert mult_idx_2[5] < mult_idx_1[7] < mult_idx_2[6]\\n448:         assert mult_idx_2[5] < mult_idx_1[8] < mult_idx_2[6]\\n449:         assert mult_idx_2[5] < mult_idx_1[9] < mult_idx_2[6]\\n450:         assert mult_idx_2[5] < mult_idx_1[10] < mult_idx_2[6]\\n451:         assert mult_idx_2[5] < mult_idx_1[11] < mult_idx_2[6]\\n452: \\n453:         indexer = mult_idx_2.get_indexer(mult_idx_1)\\n454:         expected = np.array(\\n455:             [-1, -1, -1, -1, -1, 2, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1], dtype=indexer.dtype\\n456:         )\\n457:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, indexer)\\n458: \\n459:         backfill_indexer = mult_idx_2.get_indexer(mult_idx_1, method=\\\"bfill\\\")\\n460:         expected = np.array(\\n461:             [1, 1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6], dtype=backfill_indexer.dtype\\n462:         )\\n463:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, backfill_indexer)\\n464: \\n465:         pad_indexer = mult_idx_2.get_indexer(mult_idx_1, method=\\\"pad\\\")\\n466:         expected = np.array(\\n467:             [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 2, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5], dtype=pad_indexer.dtype\\n468:         )\\n469:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, pad_indexer)\\n470: \\n471:     def test_get_indexer_crossing_levels(self):\\n472:         # https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/29896\\n473:         # tests a corner case with get_indexer() with MultiIndexes where, when we\\n474:         # need to \\\"carry\\\" across levels, proper tuple ordering is respected\\n475:         #\\n476:         # the MultiIndexes used in this test, visually, are:\\n477:         # mult_idx_1:\\n478:         #  0: 1 1 1 1\\n479:         #  1:       2\\n480:         #  2:     2 1\\n481:         #  3:       2\\n482:         #  4: 1 2 1 1\\n483:         #  5:       2\\n484:         #  6:     2 1\\n485:         #  7:       2\\n486:         #  8: 2 1 1 1\\n487:         #  9:       2\\n488:         # 10:     2 1\\n489:         # 11:       2\\n490:         # 12: 2 2 1 1\\n491:         # 13:       2\\n492:         # 14:     2 1\\n493:         # 15:       2\\n494:         #\\n495:         # mult_idx_2:\\n496:         #  0: 1 3 2 2\\n497:         #  1: 2 3 2 2\\n498:         mult_idx_1 = MultiIndex.from_product([[1, 2]] * 4)\\n499:         mult_idx_2 = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(1, 3, 2, 2), (2, 3, 2, 2)])\\n500: \\n501:         # show the tuple orderings, which get_indexer() should respect\\n502:         assert mult_idx_1[7] < mult_idx_2[0] < mult_idx_1[8]\\n503:         assert mult_idx_1[-1] < mult_idx_2[1]\\n504: \\n505:         indexer = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2)\\n506:         expected = np.array([-1, -1], dtype=indexer.dtype)\\n507:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, indexer)\\n508: \\n509:         backfill_indexer = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2, method=\\\"bfill\\\")\\n510:         expected = np.array([8, -1], dtype=backfill_indexer.dtype)\\n511:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, backfill_indexer)\\n512: \\n513:         pad_indexer = mult_idx_1.get_indexer(mult_idx_2, method=\\\"ffill\\\")\\n514:         expected = np.array([7, 15], dtype=pad_indexer.dtype)\\n515:         tm.assert_almost_equal(expected, pad_indexer)\\n516: \\n517:     def test_get_indexer_kwarg_validation(self):\\n518:         # GH#41918\\n519:         mi = MultiIndex.from_product([range(3), [\\\"A\\\", \\\"B\\\"]])\\n520: \\n521:         msg = \\\"limit argument only valid if doing pad, backfill or nearest\\\"\\n522:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\\n523:             mi.get_indexer(mi[:-1], limit=4)\\n524: \\n525:         msg = \\\"tolerance argument only valid if doing pad, backfill or nearest\\\"\\n526:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):\\n527:             mi.get_indexer(mi[:-1], tolerance=\\\"piano\\\")\\n528: \\n529:     def test_get_indexer_nan(self):\\n530:         # GH#37222\\n531:         idx1 = MultiIndex.from_product([[\\\"A\\\"], [1.0, 2.0]], names=[\\\"id1\\\", \\\"id2\\\"])\\n532:         idx2 = MultiIndex.from_product([[\\\"A\\\"], [np.nan, 2.0]], names=[\\\"id1\\\", \\\"id2\\\"])\\n533:         expected = np.array([-1, 1])\\n534:         result = idx2.get_indexer(idx1)\\n535:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected, check_dtype=False)\\n536:         result = idx1.get_indexer(idx2)\\n537:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected, check_dtype=False)\\n538: \\n539: \\n540: def test_getitem(idx):\\n541:     # scalar\\n542:     assert idx[2] == (\\\"bar\\\", \\\"one\\\")\\n543: \\n544:     # slice\\n545:     result = idx[2:5]\\n546:     expected = idx[[2, 3, 4]]\\n547:     assert result.equals(expected)\\n548: \\n549:     # boolean\\n550:     result = idx[[True, False, True, False, True, True]]\\n551:     result2 = idx[np.array([True, False, True, False, True, True])]\\n552:     expected = idx[[0, 2, 4, 5]]\\n553:     assert result.equals(expected)\\n554:     assert result2.equals(expected)\\n555: \\n556: \\n557: def test_getitem_group_select(idx):\\n558:     sorted_idx, _ = idx.sortlevel(0)\\n559:     assert sorted_idx.get_loc(\\\"baz\\\") == slice(3, 4)\\n560:     assert sorted_idx.get_loc(\\\"foo\\\") == slice(0, 2)\\n561: \\n562: \\n563: @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"ind1\\\", [[True] * 5, Index([True] * 5)])\\n564: @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n565:     \\\"ind2\\\",\\n566:     [[True, False, True, False, False], Index([True, False, True, False, False])],\\n567: )\\n568: def test_getitem_bool_index_all(ind1, ind2):\\n569:     # GH#22533\\n570:     idx = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(10, 1), (20, 2), (30, 3), (40, 4), (50, 5)])\\n571:     tm.assert_index_equal(idx[ind1], idx)\\n572: \\n573:     expected = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(10, 1), (30, 3)])\\n574:     tm.assert_index_equal(idx[ind2], expected)\\n575: \\n576: \\n577: @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"ind1\\\", [[True], Index([True])])\\n578: @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"ind2\\\", [[False], Index([False])])\\n579: def test_getitem_bool_index_single(ind1, ind2):\\n580:     # GH#22533\\n581:     idx = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(10, 1)])\\n582:     tm.assert_index_equal(idx[ind1], idx)\\n583: \\n584:     expected = MultiIndex(\\n585:         levels=[np.array([], dtype=np.int64), np.array([], dtype=np.int64)],\\n586:         codes=[[], []],\\n587:     )\\n588:     tm.assert_index_equal(idx[ind2], expected)\\n589: \\n590: \\n591: class TestGetLoc:\\n592:     def test_get_loc(self, idx):\\n593:         assert idx.get_loc((\\\"foo\\\", \\\"two\\\")) == 1\\n594:         assert idx.get_loc((\\\"baz\\\", \\\"two\\\")) == 3\\n595:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^\\\\('bar', 'two'\\\\)$\\\"):\\n596:             idx.get_loc((\\\"bar\\\", \\\"two\\\"))\\n597:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^'quux'$\\\"):\\n598:             idx.get_loc(\\\"quux\\\")\\n599: \\n600:         # 3 levels\\n601:         index = MultiIndex(\\n602:             levels=[Index(np.arange(4)), Index(np.arange(4)), Index(np.arange(4))],\\n603:             codes=[\\n604:                 np.array([0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3]),\\n605:                 np.array([0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1]),\\n606:                 np.array([1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0]),\\n607:             ],\\n608:         )\\n609:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^\\\\(1, 1\\\\)$\\\"):\\n610:             index.get_loc((1, 1))\\n611:         assert index.get_loc((2, 0)) == slice(3, 5)\\n612: \\n613:     def test_get_loc_duplicates(self):\\n614:         index = Index([2, 2, 2, 2])\\n615:         result = index.get_loc(2)\\n616:         expected = slice(0, 4)\\n617:         assert result == expected\\n618: \\n619:         index = Index([\\\"c\\\", \\\"a\\\", \\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"b\\\"])\\n620:         rs = index.get_loc(\\\"c\\\")\\n621:         xp = 0\\n622:         assert rs == xp\\n623: \\n624:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=\\\"2\\\"):\\n625:             index.get_loc(2)\\n626: \\n627:     def test_get_loc_level(self):\\n628:         index = MultiIndex(\\n629:             levels=[Index(np.arange(4)), Index(np.arange(4)), Index(np.arange(4))],\\n630:             codes=[\\n631:                 np.array([0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3]),\\n632:                 np.array([0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1]),\\n633:                 np.array([1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0]),\\n634:             ],\\n635:         )\\n636:         loc, new_index = index.get_loc_level((0, 1))\\n637:         expected = slice(1, 2)\\n638:         exp_index = index[expected].droplevel(0).droplevel(0)\\n639:         assert loc == expected\\n640:         assert new_index.equals(exp_index)\\n641: \\n642:         loc, new_index = index.get_loc_level((0, 1, 0))\\n643:         expected = 1\\n644:         assert loc == expected\\n645:         assert new_index is None\\n646: \\n647:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^\\\\(2, 2\\\\)$\\\"):\\n648:             index.get_loc_level((2, 2))\\n649:         # GH 22221: unused label\\n650:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^2$\\\"):\\n651:             index.drop(2).get_loc_level(2)\\n652:         # Unused label on unsorted level:\\n653:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^2$\\\"):\\n654:             index.drop(1, level=2).get_loc_level(2, level=2)\\n655: \\n656:         index = MultiIndex(\\n657:             levels=[[2000], list(range(4))],\\n658:             codes=[np.array([0, 0, 0, 0]), np.array([0, 1, 2, 3])],\\n659:         )\\n660:         result, new_index = index.get_loc_level((2000, slice(None, None)))\\n661:         expected = slice(None, None)\\n662:         assert result == expected\\n663:         assert new_index.equals(index.droplevel(0))\\n664: \\n665:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"dtype1\\\", [int, float, bool, str])\\n666:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"dtype2\\\", [int, float, bool, str])\\n667:     def test_get_loc_multiple_dtypes(self, dtype1, dtype2):\\n668:         # GH 18520\\n669:         levels = [np.array([0, 1]).astype(dtype1), np.array([0, 1]).astype(dtype2)]\\n670:         idx = MultiIndex.from_product(levels)\\n671:         assert idx.get_loc(idx[2]) == 2\\n672: \\n673:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"level\\\", [0, 1])\\n674:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"dtypes\\\", [[int, float], [float, int]])\\n675:     def test_get_loc_implicit_cast(self, level, dtypes):\\n676:         # GH 18818, GH 15994 : as flat index, cast int to float and vice-versa\\n677:         levels = [[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\"], [\\\"c\\\", \\\"d\\\"]]\\n678:         key = [\\\"b\\\", \\\"d\\\"]\\n679:         lev_dtype, key_dtype = dtypes\\n680:         levels[level] = np.array([0, 1], dtype=lev_dtype)\\n681:         key[level] = key_dtype(1)\\n682:         idx = MultiIndex.from_product(levels)\\n683:         assert idx.get_loc(tuple(key)) == 3\\n684: \\n685:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"dtype\\\", [bool, object])\\n686:     def test_get_loc_cast_bool(self, dtype):\\n687:         # GH 19086 : int is casted to bool, but not vice-versa (for object dtype)\\n688:         #  With bool dtype, we don't cast in either direction.\\n689:         levels = [Index([False, True], dtype=dtype), np.arange(2, dtype=\\\"int64\\\")]\\n690:         idx = MultiIndex.from_product(levels)\\n691: \\n692:         if dtype is bool:\\n693:             with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^\\\\(0, 1\\\\)$\\\"):\\n694:                 assert idx.get_loc((0, 1)) == 1\\n695:             with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^\\\\(1, 0\\\\)$\\\"):\\n696:                 assert idx.get_loc((1, 0)) == 2\\n697:         else:\\n698:             # We use python object comparisons, which treat 0 == False and 1 == True\\n699:             assert idx.get_loc((0, 1)) == 1\\n700:             assert idx.get_loc((1, 0)) == 2\\n701: \\n702:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^\\\\(False, True\\\\)$\\\"):\\n703:             idx.get_loc((False, True))\\n704:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^\\\\(True, False\\\\)$\\\"):\\n705:             idx.get_loc((True, False))\\n706: \\n707:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\\"level\\\", [0, 1])\\n708:     def test_get_loc_nan(self, level, nulls_fixture):\\n709:         # GH 18485 : NaN in MultiIndex\\n710:         levels = [[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\"], [\\\"c\\\", \\\"d\\\"]]\\n711:         key = [\\\"b\\\", \\\"d\\\"]\\n712:         levels[level] = np.array([0, nulls_fixture], dtype=type(nulls_fixture))\\n713:         key[level] = nulls_fixture\\n714:         idx = MultiIndex.from_product(levels)\\n715:         assert idx.get_loc(tuple(key)) == 3\\n716: \\n717:     def test_get_loc_missing_nan(self):\\n718:         # GH 8569\\n719:         idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays([[1.0, 2.0], [3.0, 4.0]])\\n720:         assert isinstance(idx.get_loc(1), slice)\\n721:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^3$\\\"):\\n722:             idx.get_loc(3)\\n723:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=r\\\"^nan$\\\"):\\n724:             idx.get_loc(np.nan)\\n725:         with pytest.raises(InvalidIndexError, match=r\\\"\\\\[nan\\\\]\\\"):\\n726:             # listlike/non-hashable raises TypeError\\n727:             idx.get_loc([np.nan])\\n728: \\n729:     def test_get_loc_with_values_including_missing_values(self):\\n730:         # issue 19132\\n731:         idx = MultiIndex.from_product([[np.nan, 1]] * 2)\\n732:         expected = slice(0, 2, None)\\n733:         assert idx.get_loc(np.nan) == expected\\n734: \\n735:         idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays([[np.nan, 1, 2, np.nan]])\\n736:         expected = np.array([True, False, False, True])\\n737:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(idx.get_loc(np.nan), expected)\\n738: \\n739:         idx = MultiIndex.from_product([[np.nan, 1]] * 3)\\n740:         expected = slice(2, 4, None)\\n741:         assert idx.get_loc((np.nan, 1)) == expected\\n742: \\n743:     def test_get_loc_duplicates2(self):\\n744:         # TODO: de-duplicate with test_get_loc_duplicates above?\\n745:         index = MultiIndex(\\n746:             levels=[[\\\"D\\\", \\\"B\\\", \\\"C\\\"], [0, 26, 27, 37, 57, 67, 75, 82]],\\n747:             codes=[[0, 0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2], [1, 3, 4, 6, 0, 2, 2, 3, 5, 7]],\\n748:             names=[\\\"tag\\\", \\\"day\\\"],\\n749:         )\\n750: \\n751:         assert index.get_loc(\\\"D\\\") == slice(0, 3)\\n752: \\n753:     def test_get_loc_past_lexsort_depth(self):\\n754:         # GH#30053\\n755:         idx = MultiIndex(\\n756:             levels=[[\\\"a\\\"], [0, 7], [1]],\\n757:             codes=[[0, 0], [1, 0], [0, 0]],\\n758:             names=[\\\"x\\\", \\\"y\\\", \\\"z\\\"],\\n759:             sortorder=0,\\n760:         )\\n761:         key = (\\\"a\\\", 7)\\n762: \\n763:         with tm.assert_produces_warning(PerformanceWarning):\\n764:             # PerformanceWarning: indexing past lexsort depth may impact performance\\n765:             result = idx.get_loc(key)\\n766: \\n767:         assert result == slice(0, 1, None)\\n768: \\n769:     def test_multiindex_get_loc_list_raises(self):\\n770:         # GH#35878\\n771:         idx = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(\\\"a\\\", 1), (\\\"b\\\", 2)])\\n772:         msg = r\\\"\\\\[\\\\]\\\"\\n773:         with pytest.raises(InvalidIndexError, match=msg):\\n774:             idx.get_loc([])\\n775: \\n776:     def test_get_loc_nested_tuple_raises_keyerror(self):\\n777:         # raise KeyError, not TypeError\\n778:         mi = MultiIndex.from_product([range(3), range(4), range(5), range(6)])\\n779:         key = ((2, 3, 4), \\\"foo\\\")\\n780: \\n781:         with pytest.raises(KeyError, match=re.escape(str(key))):\\n782:             mi.get_loc(key)\\n783: \\n784: \\n785: class TestWhere:\\n786:     def test_where(self):\\n787:         i = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(\\\"A\\\", 1), (\\\"A\\\", 2)])\\n788: \\n789:         msg = r\\\"\\\\.where is not supported for MultiIndex operations\\\"\\n790:         with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError, match=msg):\\n791:             i.where(True)\\n792: \\n793:     def test_where_array_like(self, listlike_box):\\n794:         mi = MultiIndex.from_tuples([(\\\"A\\\", 1), (\\\"A\\\", 2)])\\n795:         cond = [False, True]\\n796:         msg = r\\\"\\\\.where is not supported for MultiIndex operations\\\"\\n797:         with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError, match=msg):\\n798:             mi.where(listlike_box(cond))\\n799: \\n800: \\n801: class TestContains:\\n802:     def test_contains_top_level(self):\\n803:         midx = MultiIndex.from_product([[\\\"A\\\", \\\"B\\\"], [1, 2]])\\n804:         assert \\\"A\\\" in midx\\n805:         assert \\\"A\\\" not in midx._engine\\n806: \\n807:     def test_contains_with_nat(self):\\n808:         # MI with a NaT\\n809:         mi = MultiIndex(\\n810:             levels=[[\\\"C\\\"], date_range(\\\"2012-01-01\\\", periods=5)],\\n811:             codes=[[0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0], [-1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4]],\\n812:             names=[None, \\\"B\\\"],\\n813:         )\\n814:         assert (\\\"C\\\", pd.Timestamp(\\\"2012-01-01\\\")) in mi\\n815:         for val in mi.values:\\n816:             assert val in mi\\n817: \\n818:     def test_contains(self, idx):\\n819:         assert (\\\"foo\\\", \\\"two\\\") in idx\\n820:         assert (\\\"bar\\\", \\\"two\\\") not in idx\\n821:         assert None not in idx\\n822: \\n823:     def test_contains_with_missing_value(self):\\n824:         # GH#19132\\n825:         idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays([[1, np.nan, 2]])\\n826:         assert np.nan in idx\\n827: \\n828:         idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays([[1, 2], [np.nan, 3]])\\n829:         assert np.nan not in idx\\n830:         assert (1, np.nan) in idx\\n831: \\n832:     def test_multiindex_contains_dropped(self):\\n833:         # GH#19027\\n834:         # test that dropped MultiIndex levels are not in the MultiIndex\\n835:         # despite continuing to be in the MultiIndex's levels\\n836:         idx = MultiIndex.from_product([[1, 2], [3, 4]])\\n837:         assert 2 in idx\\n838:         idx = idx.drop(2)\\n839: \\n840:         # drop implementation keeps 2 in the levels\\n841:         assert 2 in idx.levels[0]\\n842:         # but it should no longer be in the index itself\\n843:         assert 2 not in idx\\n844: \\n845:         # also applies to strings\\n846:         idx = MultiIndex.from_product([[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\"], [\\\"c\\\", \\\"d\\\"]])\\n847:         assert \\\"a\\\" in idx\\n848:         idx = idx.drop(\\\"a\\\")\\n849:         assert \\\"a\\\" in idx.levels[0]\\n850:         assert \\\"a\\\" not in idx\\n851: \\n852:     def test_contains_td64_level(self):\\n853:         # GH#24570\\n854:         tx = pd.timedelta_range(\\\"09:30:00\\\", \\\"16:00:00\\\", freq=\\\"30 min\\\")\\n855:         idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays([tx, np.arange(len(tx))])\\n856:         assert tx[0] in idx\\n857:         assert \\\"element_not_exit\\\" not in idx\\n858:         assert \\\"0 day 09:30:00\\\" in idx\\n859: \\n860:     def test_large_mi_contains(self, monkeypatch):\\n861:         # GH#10645\\n862:         with monkeypatch.context():\\n863:             monkeypatch.setattr(libindex, \\\"_SIZE_CUTOFF\\\", 10)\\n864:             result = MultiIndex.from_arrays([range(10), range(10)])\\n865:             assert (10, 0) not in result\\n866: \\n867: \\n868: def test_timestamp_multiindex_indexer():\\n869:     # https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/26944\\n870:     idx = MultiIndex.from_product(\\n871:         [\\n872:             date_range(\\\"2019-01-01T00:15:33\\\", periods=100, freq=\\\"h\\\", name=\\\"date\\\"),\\n873:             [\\\"x\\\"],\\n874:             [3],\\n875:         ]\\n876:     )\\n877:     df = DataFrame({\\\"foo\\\": np.arange(len(idx))}, idx)\\n878:     result = df.loc[pd.IndexSlice[\\\"2019-1-2\\\":, \\\"x\\\", :], \\\"foo\\\"]\\n879:     qidx = MultiIndex.from_product(\\n880:         [\\n881:             date_range(\\n882:                 start=\\\"2019-01-02T00:15:33\\\",\\n883:                 end=\\\"2019-01-05T03:15:33\\\",\\n884:                 freq=\\\"h\\\",\\n885:                 name=\\\"date\\\",\\n886:             ),\\n887:             [\\\"x\\\"],\\n888:             [3],\\n889:         ]\\n890:     )\\n891:     should_be = pd.Series(data=np.arange(24, len(qidx) + 24), index=qidx, name=\\\"foo\\\")\\n892:     tm.assert_series_equal(result, should_be)\\n893: \\n894: \\n895: @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n896:     \\\"index_arr,expected,target,algo\\\",\\n897:     [\\n898:         ([[np.nan, \\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\"], [\\\"c\\\", \\\"d\\\", \\\"e\\\"]], 0, np.nan, \\\"left\\\"),\\n899:         ([[np.nan, \\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\"], [\\\"c\\\", \\\"d\\\", \\\"e\\\"]], 1, (np.nan, \\\"c\\\"), \\\"right\\\"),\\n900:         ([[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"], [\\\"d\\\", np.nan, \\\"d\\\"]], 1, (\\\"b\\\", np.nan), \\\"left\\\"),\\n901:     ],\\n902: )\\n903: def test_get_slice_bound_with_missing_value(index_arr, expected, target, algo):\\n904:     # issue 19132\\n905:     idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays(index_arr)\\n906:     result = idx.get_slice_bound(target, side=algo)\\n907:     assert result == expected\\n908: \\n909: \\n910: @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n911:     \\\"index_arr,expected,start_idx,end_idx\\\",\\n912:     [\\n913:         ([[np.nan, 1, 2], [3, 4, 5]], slice(0, 2, None), np.nan, 1),\\n914:         ([[np.nan, 1, 2], [3, 4, 5]], slice(0, 3, None), np.nan, (2, 5)),\\n915:         ([[1, 2, 3], [4, np.nan, 5]], slice(1, 3, None), (2, np.nan), 3),\\n916:         ([[1, 2, 3], [4, np.nan, 5]], slice(1, 3, None), (2, np.nan), (3, 5)),\\n917:     ],\\n918: )\\n919: def test_slice_indexer_with_missing_value(index_arr, expected, start_idx, end_idx):\\n920:     # issue 19132\\n921:     idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays(index_arr)\\n922:     result = idx.slice_indexer(start=start_idx, end=end_idx)\\n923:     assert result == expected\\n924: \\n925: \\n926: def test_pyint_engine():\\n927:     # GH#18519 : when combinations of codes cannot be represented in 64\\n928:     # bits, the index underlying the MultiIndex engine works with Python\\n929:     # integers, rather than uint64.\\n930:     N = 5\\n931:     keys = [\\n932:         tuple(arr)\\n933:         for arr in [\\n934:             [0] * 10 * N,\\n935:             [1] * 10 * N,\\n936:             [2] * 10 * N,\\n937:             [np.nan] * N + [2] * 9 * N,\\n938:             [0] * N + [2] * 9 * N,\\n939:             [np.nan] * N + [2] * 8 * N + [0] * N,\\n940:         ]\\n941:     ]\\n942:     # Each level contains 4 elements (including NaN), so it is represented\\n943:     # in 2 bits, for a total of 2*N*10 = 100 > 64 bits. If we were using a\\n944:     # 64 bit engine and truncating the first levels, the fourth and fifth\\n945:     # keys would collide; if truncating the last levels, the fifth and\\n946:     # sixth; if rotating bits rather than shifting, the third and fifth.\\n947: \\n948:     for idx, key_value in enumerate(keys):\\n949:         index = MultiIndex.from_tuples(keys)\\n950:         assert index.get_loc(key_value) == idx\\n951: \\n952:         expected = np.arange(idx + 1, dtype=np.intp)\\n953:         result = index.get_indexer([keys[i] for i in expected])\\n954:         tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected)\\n955: \\n956:     # With missing key:\\n957:     idces = range(len(keys))\\n958:     expected = np.array([-1] + list(idces), dtype=np.intp)\\n959:     missing = tuple([0, 1] * 5 * N)\\n960:     result = index.get_indexer([missing] + [keys[i] for i in idces])\\n961:     tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected)\\n962: \\n963: \\n964: @pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n965:     \\\"keys,expected\\\",\\n966:     [\\n967:         ((slice(None), [5, 4]), [1, 0]),\\n968:         ((slice(None), [4, 5]), [0, 1]),\\n969:         (([True, False, True], [4, 6]), [0, 2]),\\n970:         (([True, False, True], [6, 4]), [0, 2]),\\n971:         ((2, [4, 5]), [0, 1]),\\n972:         ((2, [5, 4]), [1, 0]),\\n973:         (([2], [4, 5]), [0, 1]),\\n974:         (([2], [5, 4]), [1, 0]),\\n975:     ],\\n976: )\\n977: def test_get_locs_reordering(keys, expected):\\n978:     # GH48384\\n979:     idx = MultiIndex.from_arrays(\\n980:         [\\n981:             [2, 2, 1],\\n982:             [4, 5, 6],\\n983:         ]\\n984:     )\\n985:     result = idx.get_locs(keys)\\n986:     expected = np.array(expected, dtype=np.intp)\\n987:     tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected)\\n988: \\n989: \\n990: def test_get_indexer_for_multiindex_with_nans(nulls_fixture):\\n991:     # GH37222\\n992:     idx1 = MultiIndex.from_product([[\\\"A\\\"], [1.0, 2.0]], names=[\\\"id1\\\", \\\"id2\\\"])\\n993:     idx2 = MultiIndex.from_product([[\\\"A\\\"], [nulls_fixture, 2.0]], names=[\\\"id1\\\", \\\"id2\\\"])\\n994: \\n995:     result = idx2.get_indexer(idx1)\\n996:     expected = np.array([-1, 1], dtype=np.intp)\\n997:     tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected)\\n998: \\n999:     result = idx1.get_indexer(idx2)\\n1000:     expected = np.array([-1, 1], dtype=np.intp)\\n1001:     tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result, expected)\\n1002: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pandas--v2.2.3@d6878b8b2a7811e59d667a6ea7414a3a3721a764/-/blob/pandas/tests/indexes/multi/test_indexing.py\"}"
